{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20年2月22日}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20年2月22日}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20年2月22日}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20年2月22日}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20年2月22日}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4年}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27年}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57年}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22年}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27th year }}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year }}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22nd year }}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

revision.text:
{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長|12=draft}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=draft}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏|12=draft}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4th year|12=draft}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|12=draft}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=draft}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦|12=draft}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27th year|12=draft}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=draft}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=draft}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22nd year|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=draft}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27th year|12=draft}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. [[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagicho|ja|左義長|12=draft}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|ja|勝山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|ja|福井県|12=draft}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=draft}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏|12=draft}} assumed control of the {{ill|Katsuyama Domain|ja|越前勝山藩|12=draft}} in {{ill|1691|ja|元禄4th year|12=draft}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|12=draft}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=draft}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|ja|三味線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinobue|ja|篠笛|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kane|ja|鉦|12=draft}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|ja|昭和27th year|12=draft}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|ja|昭和57th year|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=draft}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=draft}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|ja|平成22nd year|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=draft}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2020年10月21日 (水) 14:14 (UTC) | 一次資料 = 2020年10月21日 (水) 14:14 (UTC) }}[[画像:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|左義長ばやし]]'''勝山左義長'''（かつやまさぎちょう）は、[[福井県]][[勝山市]]で毎年2月の最終土日に行われている[[左義長]]であり、福井県を代表する[[祭|祭り]]である。[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）[[2月22日]]に福井県の[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref>。地元勝山市ではさぎっちょと呼ばれ、勝山左義長まつり実行委員会では'''勝山左義長まつり'''と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == 勝山市内では最大の祭りである。「お囃子」「絵行燈」「作り物」「押し絵」「色短冊」などが行われる。[[江戸時代]]には[[旧暦]]の[[正月]]14日・15日に行われており、[[明治]]以降は[[新暦]]の2月14日・15日になったが、その後雪の影響で、降雪量の少なくなる2月24日・25日になり、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）から現在のようになった。
=== 起源 === 古来より全国で行われている左義長だが、勝山市では[[小笠原氏]]が[[越前勝山藩]]に入封された[[1691年]]より盛んに行われた、と小笠原氏入封300年を記念したイベントからいわれ始めた。しかし、[[1603年]]（[[慶長]]8年）の文献には既に左義長についての記述がされている<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>。
=== 勝山左義長の行われている地区 === 旧[[勝山町 (福井県)|勝山町]]の、上袋田区、下袋田区、上郡区、上後区、中後区、下後区、上長渕区、下長渕区、富田区、沢区、芳野区、立川区、元町2丁目区の13地区が行い、そのうち富田区を除く12地区が高さ約6mの櫓（やぐら）を建てて行う。
=== まつりの概要 === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|勝山左義長で飾られる色短冊と御神体]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|勝山左義長のどんど焼]] ;左義長ばやし :[[三味線]]、[[笛|しの笛]]、[[鉦鼓|鉦]]で演奏される[[囃子|お囃子]]で、主に、だいづると呼ばれる「蝶よ花よ 花よのねんね まだ乳のむか 乳くびはなせ」という特徴のある囃子唄を歌い、それに合わせて'''左義長太鼓'''と呼ばれる[[太鼓]]を叩くもの。いわゆる「浮き太鼓」が特徴。太鼓の音が響かない様に、子どもや若者を座らせる。ほかにも、御大典（ごたいてん）、[[金毘羅船々|金毘羅舟々]]（こんぴらふねふね）、四調目（しちょめ）、戦友（せんゆう）などの囃子唄がある<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;絵行燈 :世相風刺・狂歌・[[行政]]・[[観光]]・[[駄洒落]]を織り交ぜて庶民の願望が[[行灯|行燈]]に描かれるもの。主に町内の[[十字路|辻]]や櫓のまわりに掛けられる。櫓の両正面下に掛かる大行燈には、狂歌を主として行政問題や世界の話題、吉祥干支にちなんだ[[川柳]]と[[絵画|絵]]が書かれる。江戸時代より始まり、藩主が「[[無礼講]]」として庶民の気持ちを、[[古川柳]]や[[狂歌]]（滑稽を詠んだ卑俗な短歌）に託すことを許したことが起源。[[1985年]]（昭和60年）から絵行燈コンクールが行われている<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;赤襦袢 :かつては櫓で赤襦袢を着られるのは、一人前の男として認められ、青年会の一員として櫓に上ることを許された証であったため、その名残で現在でも、左義長伝来の一つの儀式として、一部の太鼓を叩く若者は赤襦袢を着ている<ref name=":0" />。 ;作り物 :日常生活用具を素材に選び、その年の干支や吉祥形態にちなんだ作品を、即興で「にわか」的に作り上げるもの。主に昔の農機具や古道具などが使われる。素材や作品からもじった「シャレ」を織り込んだ、'''書き流し'''（短歌）が添えられ、一枚には、意義（意味・わけ）を書き、もう一枚には、作品の素材と作り物を洒落言葉で表現する。[[1952年]]（昭和27年）から勝山商工会議所主催で作り物コンクールが行われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;押し絵 :綿をつめた布張りの飾り物。過去には女性の花嫁修業として盛んに行われていた。 ;色短冊 :色のついた[[短冊]]を祭りを行う地区に飾り付けるもの。[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に祭りの1週間前から飾られるようになった。江戸時代から明治の中ごろまでは、二尺程の松の小枝に赤い紙の短冊をくくりつけ、各家々の軒先に飾られるというものだった。現在の短冊は、明治のおわりごろ、五色組飾り纏の色に変えられたと伝えられ、これは[[1845年]]（[[弘化]]2年）8代藩主[[小笠原長守]]のときに青・黄・赤・白・黒の五組に編成された火消の制度に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。昭和55年までは地区ごとに色が違っていたが、上袋田と下袋田を除いて緑・黄・赤の短冊になった。下袋田は白・緑・黄・赤、上袋田は白・青・赤となっている。 ;[[左義長|どんど焼き]] :各町内にある[[神体|御神体]]（松飾り）を[[九頭竜川|弁天河原]]に集めて一斉に点火するもの。2日目の夜に行われる。[[1988年]]（昭和60年）頃から神事としてどんど焼きに御神火送りが行われるようになった。左義長は鎮火祭とも深く関係するため、火祭りもあわせて、神明神社に祀られている[[カグツチ|火産霊神]]の御火を「御神火」として頂く。かつてはどんど焼きの翌朝に、'''後燃やし'''の残り火で、粥を炊いて家族などに配ったり、炙った餅などを持ち帰って食べたり、灰を体に塗ったりすることで無病息災を願ったといわれる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。
== 関連項目 == *[[勝山城博物館]] - 櫓が展示されており、左義長太鼓を叩く体験ができる。 *[[椎名誠]] -「ニッポンありゃまあお祭り紀行」にて勝山左義長を絶賛し、[[2010年]]（平成22年）[[2月27日]]に勝山市より「かつやま左義長大使」に委嘱された。 *[[えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。]]- 主人公が左義長太鼓を叩く様子が描かれている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク== * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ 勝山左義長] - 勝山市 * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ 勝山観光ナビ 勝山左義長] - 勝山市観光まちづくり * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ 勝山左義長ばやし保存会] * {{Facebook|sagichou|勝山左義長まつり}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつやまさきちよう}} [[Category:福井県の祭り]] [[Category:勝山市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[勝山左義長]] to [[Katsuyama Sagicho]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 出典の明記 = 2020年10月21日 (水) 14:14 (UTC) | 一次資料 = 2020年10月21日 (水) 14:14 (UTC) }}[[画像:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|左義長ばやし]]'''勝山左義長'''（かつやまさぎちょう）は、[[福井県]][[勝山市]]で毎年2月の最終土日に行われている[[左義長]]であり、福井県を代表する[[祭|祭り]]である。[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）[[2月22日]]に福井県の[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref>。地元勝山市ではさぎっちょと呼ばれ、勝山左義長まつり実行委員会では'''勝山左義長まつり'''と呼ばれる。
== 概要 == 勝山市内では最大の祭りである。「お囃子」「絵行燈」「作り物」「押し絵」「色短冊」などが行われる。[[江戸時代]]には[[旧暦]]の[[正月]]14日・15日に行われており、[[明治]]以降は[[新暦]]の2月14日・15日になったが、その後雪の影響で、降雪量の少なくなる2月24日・25日になり、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）から現在のようになった。
=== 起源 === 古来より全国で行われている左義長だが、勝山市では[[小笠原氏]]が[[越前勝山藩]]に入封された[[1691年]]より盛んに行われた、と小笠原氏入封300年を記念したイベントからいわれ始めた。しかし、[[1603年]]（[[慶長]]8年）の文献には既に左義長についての記述がされている<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>。
=== 勝山左義長の行われている地区 === 旧[[勝山町 (福井県)|勝山町]]の、上袋田区、下袋田区、上郡区、上後区、中後区、下後区、上長渕区、下長渕区、富田区、沢区、芳野区、立川区、元町2丁目区の13地区が行い、そのうち富田区を除く12地区が高さ約6mの櫓（やぐら）を建てて行う。
=== まつりの概要 === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|勝山左義長で飾られる色短冊と御神体]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|勝山左義長のどんど焼]] ;左義長ばやし :[[三味線]]、[[笛|しの笛]]、[[鉦鼓|鉦]]で演奏される[[囃子|お囃子]]で、主に、だいづると呼ばれる「蝶よ花よ 花よのねんね まだ乳のむか 乳くびはなせ」という特徴のある囃子唄を歌い、それに合わせて'''左義長太鼓'''と呼ばれる[[太鼓]]を叩くもの。いわゆる「浮き太鼓」が特徴。太鼓の音が響かない様に、子どもや若者を座らせる。ほかにも、御大典（ごたいてん）、[[金毘羅船々|金毘羅舟々]]（こんぴらふねふね）、四調目（しちょめ）、戦友（せんゆう）などの囃子唄がある<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;絵行燈 :世相風刺・狂歌・[[行政]]・[[観光]]・[[駄洒落]]を織り交ぜて庶民の願望が[[行灯|行燈]]に描かれるもの。主に町内の[[十字路|辻]]や櫓のまわりに掛けられる。櫓の両正面下に掛かる大行燈には、狂歌を主として行政問題や世界の話題、吉祥干支にちなんだ[[川柳]]と[[絵画|絵]]が書かれる。江戸時代より始まり、藩主が「[[無礼講]]」として庶民の気持ちを、[[古川柳]]や[[狂歌]]（滑稽を詠んだ卑俗な短歌）に託すことを許したことが起源。[[1985年]]（昭和60年）から絵行燈コンクールが行われている<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;赤襦袢 :かつては櫓で赤襦袢を着られるのは、一人前の男として認められ、青年会の一員として櫓に上ることを許された証であったため、その名残で現在でも、左義長伝来の一つの儀式として、一部の太鼓を叩く若者は赤襦袢を着ている<ref name=":0" />。 ;作り物 :日常生活用具を素材に選び、その年の干支や吉祥形態にちなんだ作品を、即興で「にわか」的に作り上げるもの。主に昔の農機具や古道具などが使われる。素材や作品からもじった「シャレ」を織り込んだ、'''書き流し'''（短歌）が添えられ、一枚には、意義（意味・わけ）を書き、もう一枚には、作品の素材と作り物を洒落言葉で表現する。[[1952年]]（昭和27年）から勝山商工会議所主催で作り物コンクールが行われている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。 ;押し絵 :綿をつめた布張りの飾り物。過去には女性の花嫁修業として盛んに行われていた。 ;色短冊 :色のついた[[短冊]]を祭りを行う地区に飾り付けるもの。[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に祭りの1週間前から飾られるようになった。江戸時代から明治の中ごろまでは、二尺程の松の小枝に赤い紙の短冊をくくりつけ、各家々の軒先に飾られるというものだった。現在の短冊は、明治のおわりごろ、五色組飾り纏の色に変えられたと伝えられ、これは[[1845年]]（[[弘化]]2年）8代藩主[[小笠原長守]]のときに青・黄・赤・白・黒の五組に編成された火消の制度に由来する<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。昭和55年までは地区ごとに色が違っていたが、上袋田と下袋田を除いて緑・黄・赤の短冊になった。下袋田は白・緑・黄・赤、上袋田は白・青・赤となっている。 ;[[左義長|どんど焼き]] :各町内にある[[神体|御神体]]（松飾り）を[[九頭竜川|弁天河原]]に集めて一斉に点火するもの。2日目の夜に行われる。[[1988年]]（昭和60年）頃から神事としてどんど焼きに御神火送りが行われるようになった。左義長は鎮火祭とも深く関係するため、火祭りもあわせて、神明神社に祀られている[[カグツチ|火産霊神]]の御火を「御神火」として頂く。かつてはどんど焼きの翌朝に、'''後燃やし'''の残り火で、粥を炊いて家族などに配ったり、炙った餅などを持ち帰って食べたり、灰を体に塗ったりすることで無病息災を願ったといわれる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>。
== 関連項目 == *[[勝山城博物館]] - 櫓が展示されており、左義長太鼓を叩く体験ができる。 *[[椎名誠]] -「ニッポンありゃまあお祭り紀行」にて勝山左義長を絶賛し、[[2010年]]（平成22年）[[2月27日]]に勝山市より「かつやま左義長大使」に委嘱された。 *[[えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。]]- 主人公が左義長太鼓を叩く様子が描かれている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク== * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ 勝山左義長] - 勝山市 * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ 勝山観光ナビ 勝山左義長] - 勝山市観光まちづくり * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ 勝山左義長ばやし保存会] * {{Facebook|sagichou|勝山左義長まつり}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かつやまさきちよう}} [[Category:福井県の祭り]] [[Category:勝山市の歴史]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|qq=|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|qq=|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|qq=|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|勝山左義長|version=97918884|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|qq=|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|qq=|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|qq=|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|qq=|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>

On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}
