[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

<!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--> [[Category:History of Katsuyama]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}} {{wikidata link|Q11400243}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

<!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--><!--Katsuyama Sagicho--> [[Category:History of Katsuyama]] [[Category:Festivals in Fukui Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}} {{wikidata link|Q11400243}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a [[:en:Sagichō Fire Festival|Sagicho]] festival held annually on the last weekend of February in [[:en:Katsuyama, Fukui|Katsuyama, Fukui]], [[:en:Fukui Prefecture|Fukui Prefecture]]. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since [[:en:1982|Showa 57]], the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]] assumed control of the [[:en:Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|Katsuyama Domain]] in [[:en:1691|1691]]. However, a document from [[:en:1603|1603]] already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using [[:en:Shamisen|Shamisen]], [[:en:Shinobue|Shinobue]], and [[:en:Kane (instrument)|Kane]], the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since [[:en:1952|Showa 27]], a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by [[:en:1982|Showa 57]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in [[:en:2010|Heisei 22]]. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400243]] Wikidata name: Katsuyama Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagicho}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|en|Katsuyama, Fukui|WD=Q847543|lt=Katsuyama, Fukui}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}} assumed control of the {{ill|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|en|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|WD=Q10863286|lt=Katsuyama Domain}} in {{ill|1691|en|1691|WD=Q7682|lt=1691}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagicho}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|en|Katsuyama, Fukui|WD=Q847543|lt=Katsuyama, Fukui}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}} assumed control of the {{ill|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|en|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|WD=Q10863286|lt=Katsuyama Domain}} in {{ill|1691|en|1691|WD=Q7682|lt=1691}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|勝山左義長|version=97918884|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:勝山左義長]]

The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also =="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]]
{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagicho}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|en|Katsuyama, Fukui|WD=Q847543|lt=Katsuyama, Fukui}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}} assumed control of the {{ill|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|en|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|WD=Q10863286|lt=Katsuyama Domain}} in {{ill|1691|en|1691|WD=Q7682|lt=1691}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Echitetsu Monogatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|勝山左義長|version=97918884|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:勝山左義長]]

=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Q11400255|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen|1=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue|1=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane|1=Kane (instrument)}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22|1=2010}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Q60997704|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Katsuyama Sagicho Festival]][[ja:勝山左義長]]
{{Multiple issues | Citations needed = October 21, 2020 | Primary sources = October 21, 2020 }}
[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagicho|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|en|Katsuyama, Fukui|WD=Q847543|lt=Katsuyama, Fukui|1=Katsuyama, Fukui}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}} assumed control of the {{ill|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|en|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|WD=Q10863286|lt=Katsuyama Domain|1=Echizen-Katsuyama Domain}} in {{ill|1691|en|1691|WD=Q7682|lt=1691|1=1691}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603|1=1603}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Q11400255|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen|1=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue|1=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane|1=Kane (instrument)}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>

[[File:kaminaga.jpg|thumb|350px|The "Sagicho Bayashi" music performance]] '''Katsuyama Sagicho''' (勝山左義長) is a {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|WD=Q9332740|lt=Sagicho|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} festival held annually on the last weekend of February in {{ill|Katsuyama, Fukui|en|Katsuyama, Fukui|WD=Q847543|lt=Katsuyama, Fukui|1=Katsuyama, Fukui}}, {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|WD=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is one of the representative festivals of Fukui Prefecture. On {{ill|February 22, 2008|ja|平成20th year 2月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 22, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Fukui Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/mukeimin/katsuyama-sagicho.html |title=福井県の文化財「勝山左義長」 |publisher=福井県 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> Locally referred to as "Sagiccho," it is officially named '''Katsuyama Sagicho Festival''' by the festival's organizing committee.
== Overview == The Katsuyama Sagicho is the largest festival in Katsuyama City. Key elements include "Ohayashi" music performances, "Eandon" lantern displays, "Tsukurimono" sculptures, "Oshie" pressed pictures, and "Irotanzaku" colored paper strips. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was held on the 14th and 15th days of the {{ill|lunar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s New Year. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the festival was shifted to February 14 and 15 of the solar calendar but was later moved to February 24 and 25 due to heavy snowfalls. Since {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}, the festival has been held on the last weekend of February.
=== Origins === Sagicho festivals have been held across Japan for centuries. In Katsuyama, it became popular after the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}} assumed control of the {{ill|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|en|Echizen-Katsuyama Domain|WD=Q10863286|lt=Katsuyama Domain|1=Echizen-Katsuyama Domain}} in {{ill|1691|en|1691|WD=Q7682|lt=1691|1=1691}}. However, a document from {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603|1=1603}} already mentions the festival.<ref>勝山町史稿説</ref>
=== Districts Participating in Katsuyama Sagicho === The festival is held in 13 districts of the former {{ill|Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|ja|勝山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Town (Fukui)|1=Q11400255|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400255}}: Kamibukuroda, Shimobukuroda, Kamigori, Kamigo, Nakago, Shimogo, Kamilongafuchi, Shimolongafuchi, Tomita, Sawa, Yoshino, Tachikawa, and Motomachi 2-chome. Of these, 12 districts, excluding Tomita, construct 6-meter-tall "yagura" towers for the festival.
=== Festival Highlights === [[File:Katsuyama Sagicho tanzaku.jpg|thumb|250px|Irotanzaku and sacred objects at Katsuyama Sagicho]] [[File:Katsuyama DondoYaki.JPG|thumb|250px|Dondo-Yaki at Katsuyama Sagicho]] ; Sagicho Bayashi Performed using {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|WD=Q241337|lt=Shamisen|1=Shamisen}}, {{ill|Shinobue|en|Shinobue|WD=Q3100072|lt=Shinobue|1=Shinobue}}, and {{ill|Kane (instrument)|en|Kane (instrument)|WD=Q2280552|lt=Kane|1=Kane (instrument)}}, the "Ohayashi" features distinctive festival songs, including the "Daidzuru" song and "Sagicho Taiko" drumming. The drum is struck in a unique "floating" style. Other songs include "Gotaiken," "Konpira Fune Fune," "Shichome," and "Senyu."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about06.html|title=左義長太鼓・左義長ばやし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Eandon These lanterns feature satirical commentary, local concerns, and humorous poems, displayed around yagura towers and intersections. Large eandon include drawings and poems, originating from a tradition allowing commoners to express their sentiments freely.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about07.html|title=赤襦袢・絵行燈 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Akajuban Traditionally, wearing a red undergarment signified a young man's coming of age. Today, it remains part of the festival's traditions.<ref name=":0" /> ; Tsukurimono Improvised sculptures crafted from everyday objects, often with humorous or symbolic meanings. Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|WD=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22|1=2010}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Q60997704|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/ Katsuyama Sagicho Official Website] * [https://katsuyama-navi.jp/sagiccho/ Katsuyama Tourism Navi: Katsuyama Sagicho] * [http://sagicho-hozonkai.moo.jp/ Katsuyama Sagicho Preservation Society] * {{Facebook|sagichou|Katsuyama Sagicho Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|勝山左義長|version=97918884|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:勝山左義長]]

Since {{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}, a Tsukurimono contest has been held.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about08.html|title=作り物・書き流し - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Oshie Decorative pressed fabric ornaments, historically part of bridal training for women. ; Irotanzaku Colored strips of paper used as decorations in participating districts. The tradition dates back to the Edo period, evolving into its modern form by {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=Showa 57|1=1982}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about05.html|title=色短冊 - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref> ; Dondo-Yaki On the second night, sacred decorations are burned at Benten Riverbank in a fire ceremony. This ritual is connected to Shinto purification practices.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katsuyama.fukui.jp/kankou/sagityo/about/about09.html|title=どんど焼き・後燃やし - 勝山左義長について|accessdate=2020-10-21|publisher=勝山市}}</ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Katsuyama Castle Museum|ja|勝山城博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuyama Castle Museum|1=Katsuyama Castle Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400239}} - Features exhibits on Sagicho, including drumming experiences. * {{ill|Makoto Shiina|ja|椎名誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Shiina|1=Makoto Shiina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11540829}} - Praised the festival in his work, becoming a "Katsuyama Sagicho Ambassador" in {{ill|2010|en|2010|qid=Q1995|lt=Heisei 22|1=2010}}. * {{ill|Echitetsu Monogatari|ja|えちてつ物語〜わたし、故郷に帰ってきました。|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echitetsu Monogatari|1=Q60997704|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60997704}} - Features Sagicho drum scenes.
